subject: Statistics
title: Word Frequencies: Zipf's Law
description: A quick win: understand Word Frequencies: Zipf's Law.

hook.text: The word 'the' appears twice as often as 'of,' three times as often as 'and.' Why?

buildup.text: Zipf's law says the nth most common word appears roughly 1/n as often as the most common.

discovery.text: It holds in every human language, and extends to city sizes, website visits, and music plays.

twist.text: Nobody fully understands why. Decades of research and Zipf's law remains an unsolved mystery.

climax.text: A few words do most of the work. A few cities hold most people. Concentration is universal.

punchline.text: The popular dominate. Zipf saw it in every dataset.

quiz.question: What does Zipf's law describe?
options: The nth most frequent item occurs at roughly 1/n the rate of the most frequent
Words are used equally often across languages
Rare words are more important than common ones
